context.description: A reflective prose section argues that consciousness and emotion do not fully control human action, using the image of a hidden supercomputer of bodily self-maintenance. It introduces homeostasis as the body's concealed regulatory system, maintaining temperature, blood pressure, hormones, sleep, nutrients, and acid-base balance. The page frames these subsystems as essential to survival and often unnoticed until they fail.
normalization_notes: Translated the German running text to English for retrieval.
No explicit chapter number visible; preserved the section heading only.

fields.section_heading: Homeostasis: The wonderful world of the hidden supercomputer - superior self-preservation

rows.heading_text: Homeostasis: The wonderful world of the hidden supercomputer - superior self-preservation
rows.text_content: A reflective section on homeostasis and bodily self-regulation.

rows.paragraph_text: The author contrasts conscious will with emotional and bodily systems, arguing that inner regulation often drives behavior beyond awareness.
rows.text_content: Conscious will, emotion, and hidden bodily regulation.

rows.paragraph_text: The body keeps temperature within a narrow range even in extreme external heat or cold, showing complex regulation beneath the surface.
rows.text_content: Temperature homeostasis from 36.5 to 37.5 degrees Celsius.

rows.paragraph_text: Sixteen subsystems are named as supporting homeostasis, including osmoregulation, blood pressure, hormones, thermal and energy balance, nutrient balance, sleep regulation, and acid-base balance.
The text warns that when this balance is disturbed, serious illness or death can follow.
rows.text_content: Failure of homeostasis can be fatal.
